title: SQL Basics Practice Pack
description: Small built-in commerce and support dataset for practicing SELECT, WHERE, JOIN, GROUP BY, and review flows without connecting to a production database.

tables.name: customers
tables.description: Customer accounts used for join and filtering practice.

tables.name: orders
tables.description: Order facts for filtering, joining, and aggregation practice.

tables.name: support_tickets
tables.description: Support requests for status and priority filtering practice.

columns.name: opened_date
columns.description: Ticket open date in ISO format

examples.title: Show paid orders
examples.sql: SELECT id, customer_id, total_amount FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid' ORDER BY id

examples.title: Join customers and orders
examples.sql: SELECT customers.name, orders.total_amount FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id ORDER BY orders.id

problems.title: List all customers
problems.prompt: Return each customer id, name, and region ordered by id.
problems.hint: Start from the customers table and select only the requested columns.
problems.expected_sql: SELECT id, name, region FROM customers ORDER BY id

problems.title: Find high-value paid orders
problems.prompt: Return order id, customer_id, and total_amount for paid orders with total_amount at least 100000, ordered by total_amount descending.
problems.expected_sql: SELECT id, customer_id, total_amount FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid' AND total_amount >= 100000 ORDER BY total_amount DESC

problems.title: Join paid orders to customers
problems.prompt: Return customer name, order id, and total_amount for paid orders ordered by order id.
problems.expected_sql: SELECT customers.name, orders.id, orders.total_amount FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id WHERE orders.status = 'paid' ORDER BY orders.id

problems.title: Summarize paid orders by region
problems.prompt: Return region, paid order count, and paid total amount by region ordered by region.
problems.hint: Join customers to orders, filter paid orders, then group by region.
problems.expected_sql: SELECT customers.region, COUNT(orders.id) AS order_count, SUM(orders.total_amount) AS total_amount FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id WHERE orders.status = 'paid' GROUP BY customers.region ORDER BY customers.region

problems.title: Find open high priority tickets
problems.prompt: Return ticket id and customer_id for open high priority tickets ordered by ticket id.
problems.hint: Use support_tickets and filter both priority and status.
problems.expected_sql: SELECT id, customer_id FROM support_tickets WHERE status = 'open' AND priority = 'high' ORDER BY id
